title: Assault on Doniphon

{{Quote|Rebels. They were dug into the Selpathian Hills, low on ammunition. We were on the plain, manning the AV-9. Somehow they got a transmission through the jamming. The rebels at Damatua sent starfighters.|[[Beilert Valance/Legends|Beilert Valance]] to [[4A8-RA-7]]|<ref name="TEGW:AC" />}}
The '''assault on Doniphon''' was part of the [[Kwymar Suppressions/Legends|Kwymar Suppressions]] in [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]]. Rebel forces on [[Doniphon]] were defeated by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].

==Background==
In [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]],<ref name="TEGW:AC" /> the Rebel bases in the [[Kwymar sector/Legends|Kwymar sector]] came under attack by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces in a campaign known as the [[Kwymar Suppressions/Legends|Kwymar Suppressions]]. [[Doniphon]] was one of those [[Planet/Legends|planets]] attacked.<ref name="Valance1" />

==The battle==
[[File:Battle_of_Doniphon.jpg|thumb|left|150px|[[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] attacked the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]].]]

During the battle, many [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] were strafed by Rebel [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]]. One of the injured officers was [[Sergeant major/Legends|Sergeant Major]] [[Beilert Valance/Legends|Beilert Valance]].<ref name="Valance2" /> His [[Platoon/Legends|platoon]] was struck by a [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedo]].<ref name="TCSWE">''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''</ref> Despite those heavy casualties,<ref name="Valance2" /> The Imperial forces were eventually victorious.<ref name="TEA" />

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|But that's the past... I've destroyed that!|[[Beilert Valance/Legends|Beilert Valance]]|<ref name="SW16" />}}
After the battle, a badly wounded Beilert Valance was left at [[Anglebay Station]] on [[Telos IV]]. The medics there saved his life, but only by replacing half of his body with [[Cybernetics/Legends|cybernetics]].<ref name="Valance2" />

Years later, Beilert Valance, now turned [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]], [[Attack on Anglebay Station|attacked]] and destroyed the Anglebay Station in order to erase all traces of his transformation.<ref name="Valance2" />

==Behind the scenes==
The planet where Valance was injured in [[Star Wars (1977) 16|''Star Wars'' (1977) 16]] was [[Retroactive continuity|retconned]] as Doniphon by author [[Jason Fry]] in the miniature scenarios ''[[The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale]]''. Fry named the planet after Tom Doniphon, [[John Wayne]]'s character in the [[1960s|1962]] western movie ''The Man Who Shot Liberty Valance''.

In [[Star Wars: The Essential Guide to Warfare Author's Cut|''The Essential Guide to Warfare ''Author's Cut]], Valance describes the enemy fighters as "''Split-wings. ARC-170s, maybe. Or some kind of Z-95. HUD never painted them with a positive ID.''" This is likely an attempt to reconcile the depiction of X-Wings in the battle with later media establishing that X-Wings were not produced until circa 1 BBY.
